title: Wall Street Is Wrong About This Quantum Computing Stock
why:
- IonQ reported 202% revenue growth last year, reaching $130 million in sales.
- Management forecasts revenue of $225 to $245 million in 2026, representing 81% growth at the midpoint.
- IonQ has integrations with Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud Platform, and a strategic partnership with Nvidia.
- The company has spent over $4 billion on acquisitions in recent years, with much of its revenue and growth coming from these acquired assets.
- IonQ posted net losses of over $500 million in 2025 and negative operating cash flow of $283 million.
- Since 2024, IonQ’s outstanding share count has almost doubled due to significant stock issuances to raise money for acquisitions and operations.
